name: Memphis Minnie
description: American blues guitarist, singer, and songwriter (1897–1973)
abstract: Lizzie Douglas (June 3, 1897 – August 6, 1973), better known as Memphis Minnie, was a blues guitarist, vocalist, and songwriter whose recording career lasted for over three decades. She recorded around 200 songs, some of the best known being "When the Levee Breaks", "Me and My Chauffeur Blues", "Bumble Bee", and "Nothing in Rambling".

about_quotes.speaker: Mose Allison
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I think Memphis Minnie had a song that you liked. [INTERVIEWEE] “Doin’ The Bo’hog Grind” or something.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mose Allison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Will Shade and Dewey Corley
about_quotes.evidence: Memphis Minnie is sick. She got a stroke. She can't talk, can't walk in a wheelchair. Gee, that's terrible.
about_quotes.doc_title: Will Shade and Dewey Corley Interview, 1960-05-25

about_quotes.speaker: Ann Rabson
about_quotes.evidence: Memphis Minnie were the only two who I could look to and say these people, these women play guitar.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ann Rabson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 21, 2007
